guest: Multiple investors (Doug Leone, Bill Ackman, Bill Gurley, Orlando Bravo, Marcello Claw, et al.)
guest_role: VCs/growth/buyout investors discussing valuation frameworks across fund stages
assertiveness_why: Harry actively curates and frames pricing debate; asks probing follow-ups and connects divergent viewpoints. More assertive than passive moderator but not adversarial.
question_style: Harry stitches investor clips thematically, asking targeted follow-ups that push guests to clarify frameworks. Less interrogative than connective—he's building narrative threads across multiple perspectives rather than challenging individuals.
tone: Professional, neutral curator. Harry steps back to let investors speak but guides the overall argument structure. Businesslike rather than warm.
depth: Very strategic. Digs into pricing across funnel stages, market structure theory (Glengarry Glen Ross winner-takes-most), ownership vs. cost-of-capital math, portfolio-level risk, and scenario modeling. Tracks how frameworks differ between seed and late-stage investing.
harry_quotes: Can I ask where it doesn't matter?
How do you feel about that?
Do you still want to get involved?
I want your feedback on how we can make the show better for you.
learnings: Market size matters more than entry price when opportunity is genuinely massive; founders should focus on huge TAMs first, unit economics second
Price sensitivity must increase the further down the funnel you invest; seed can be loose, but series B-D requires discipline or valuations compound into bad returns
Ownership percentage heuristics (20%) obscure the real goal: cost-of-capital returns. A smaller piece of a 100-billion-dollar exit beats a larger piece of a dead company
Work backwards from target returns (typically 25%+ in 5 years) to determine fair entry price; this avoids FOMO-driven overpayment
Market structure dominance (winner-takes-most in many tech verticals) means you should only pay premium prices for founders/companies likely to capture category leadership
Portfolio-level price awareness matters more than deal-by-deal rigidity; consistency in entry multiples matters, but individual outliers can still work if conviction is extreme
notable: Compilation episode featuring 12+ legendary VCs (Leone, Ackman, Gurley, Bravo, Claw, Perak, Escobarri, Fishner-Wolfson, Vanister, Gerstner, Tisch, George) discussing price frameworks. Harry serves as curator/moderator rather than traditional interviewer. Format weaves together thematic clips into narrative debate.
